full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|2.16|> What exactly is there to be grateful for on this strange Thanksgiving Day?<|9.28|> <|10.48|> The skeptic inside of me has posed this question for a few weeks now.<|14.48|> <|15.36|> It's a fair question, an honest question, a legitimate question.<|20.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What exactly is there to be thankful for on this strange Thanksgiving day?<|7.00|> <|7.00|> You're either watching this service from home or in an airport terminal or from an Airbnb you and other cautious loved ones have rented at a point between their city and yours.<|19.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> You're watching this service in the middle of a deadly global pandemic<|5.00|> <|5.00|> that is pushing the limits of our social safety nets, emotional strength, and for some, financial resources.<|15.00|> <|15.00|> You have wrestled for months trying to imagine what this day will look like without the whole family in one place, at one time.<|25.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> grandchildren and cousins scattered across the country,<|3.94|> <|3.94|> rent asunder by fear and conspiracy theories.<|8.38|> <|9.38|> You aren't buying as many turkeys this year.<|12.82|> <|12.82|> My dad will appreciate that.<|14.54|> <|15.52|> You aren't hosting as many guests this year.<|19.02|> <|20.08|> This is Thanksgiving at its slimmest and most limited.<|25.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It is very possible that you come to this Thanksgiving Day with dread for what Christmas holds.<|6.88|> <|7.64|> Or you could be coming to this Thanksgiving Day sighing in relief that you don't have to see that uncle or that cousin.<|16.02|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Wherever you find yourself this day, I am happy you are joining this cathedral community around the essentials of Christian faith. The Greek word here can also be translated middle, midst, border, distance.<|15.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Jesus is traveling through a middle place, a region between Samaria,<|6.18|> <|6.18|> which is the land belonging to people his own people despised,<|11.14|> <|11.14|> and Galilee, territory occupied by Rome.<|16.50|> <|17.50|> In other words, Jesus is on the border between where he's been,<|22.52|> <|23.44|> where he'd rather not go, and where he is going.<|28.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> How many of you find yourself in that middle place today,<|5.00|> <|5.00|> where you've been, where you'd rather not go,<|8.00|> <|8.00|> and where you are going?<|11.00|> <|11.00|> In verse 11, Jesus enters a village<|15.00|> <|15.00|> and is greeted by 10 lepers.<|18.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Please notice that social distancing did not originate with us.<|5.00|> <|5.00|> The writer goes to great lengths to emphasize that the lepers, quote, "kept their distance."<|12.00|> <|12.00|> One translation has the lepers afar off, lest we get too concerned that they're violating COVID protocols.<|22.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The 10 lepers are at a distance, afar off because of very specific restrictions handed down by the law of Moses.<|9.64|> <|10.84|> Quote, should a person have on the skin of his body an inflammation, he shall be declared unclean, end quote.<|19.62|> <|20.06|> And that's from Leviticus 13.<|21.74|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.88|> About this, one scholar says, quote,<|4.06|> <|4.06|> "The quarantining of the person afflicted with leprosy<|8.74|> <|8.74|> might involve a fear of contagion in the medical sense,<|12.74|> <|12.74|> or might be chiefly an avoidance of ritual contamination.<|16.84|> <|16.84|> And one suspects that the two were blurred<|20.16|> <|20.16|> in the Israelite imagination."<|22.74|> <|22.74|> End quote.<|23.74|> <|24.94|> Familiar.<|26.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The death of so many in our own time from COVID-19 has been defined by isolation and near banishment,<|9.00|> <|9.00|> which may very well be one of the most heartbreaking things to look back on once this pandemic ends.<|16.00|> <|16.00|> And yes, this pandemic will end.<|19.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Lepers were usually quarantined together in a place that the Bible refers to as outside the gate,<|9.72|> <|9.72|> a designated place at a distance and afar off, not unlike the incarcerated of our time.<|18.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.20|> In verse 13, when they cry,<|4.08|> <|4.08|> "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us,"<|7.98|> <|7.98|> they are not just making an eloquent theological statement,<|11.88|> <|11.88|> they are asking to be restored to the community,<|15.52|> <|16.42|> to be able to see their loved ones again.<|18.46|> <|19.32|> They're asking to be made whole,<|21.34|> <|21.34|> because I am only human through the humanity<|26.34|> <|26.84|> of other people.<|28.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.46|> Jesus has mercy on them, all 10 of them,<|6.18|> <|6.18|> and only one returns to praise God<|10.36|> <|10.36|> and offer thanks for their healing.<|13.12|> <|14.80|> The other nine, I suspect,<|17.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> were off observing the mayor's new restrictions.<|5.00|> <|5.38|> No more than 10 people from different households<|8.54|> <|8.54|> can gather in level three.<|10.92|> <|11.96|> Humor aside, who is to say<|16.00|> <|16.00|> that the nine were themselves not grateful?<|19.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.04|> Yes, we know the one was,<|4.54|> <|4.54|> and it is notable that he is a Samaritan.<|7.40|> <|7.40|> Millions of sermons have been preached<|9.38|> <|9.38|> about those two things.<|11.54|> <|13.04|> A person Jesus' original audience as a Samaritan<|17.18|> <|17.18|> would associate with all that is wrong in the world.<|20.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The surprising figure, the one most afar off, at the greatest distance, is thankful.<|11.00|> <|11.00|> But the nine, those mysterious characters we don't hear of again, I wouldn't count them<|19.12|> <|19.12|> out.<|20.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Maybe they were too busy reconnecting with their loved ones or traveling again<|8.00|> <|8.00|> or seeing that friend they haven't seen in a long time<|12.00|> <|12.00|> or hugging the niece they haven't seen since she was an infant.<|17.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Maybe, in some way, they are an eschatological reality for us,<|7.00|> <|7.00|> a shaft of light from the future that says to us,<|13.00|> <|13.00|> "Our faith has made us well, and it will make you well too.<|21.00|> <|21.00|> It will make the world well." Amen.<|28.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|3.00|><|endoftext|>
